mnemonics.title: Menu Creation Steps
mnemonics.content: C-C-A-A-A: Create menu (glutCreateMenu), Create entries (glutAddMenuEntry), Attach to button (glutAttachMenu). Remember: 'C'reate 'C'allback, 'A'dd entries, 'A'ttach button.

mnemonics.title: GLUT Menu Functions
mnemonics.content: Create Add Attach Destroy Detach - CAADD: Create (menu), Add (entries/submenu), Attach (to button), Destroy (when done), Detach (when needed).

mnemonics.title: Menu Callback Remember
mnemonics.content: When you SELECT, you GET a VALUE - Callback receives the VALUE from the menu entry you selected.

acronyms.usage: Window system-independent API for creating menus, windows, and handling input in OpenGL applications.
The visual elements like menus that allow users to interact with graphics applications.
Architecture pattern often used with menu-driven graphics applications to separate concerns.

visualTips.visualization: Think of menu creation as building a tree: Root menu at top, branches are submenus, leaves are menu entries. Attach to button at the root.
When you click a menu item, imagine a value traveling from menu → callback function like a letter being delivered to a specific house (the function) with an address number (the value).

analogies.analogy: Just like a restaurant menu has sections (appetizers = submenus) and items (entries), GLUT menus organize options hierarchically. When you order (select), the chef (callback) prepares your request based on what you chose (value).
Attaching a menu to a button is like giving a restaurant pager to a customer - when they press the button (right-click), the menu 'vibrates' and appears for them to make a selection.
